title: WAN i2v stage-2 runs on a hardcoded seed 0 (Input_Seed orphaned)
description:
Found by the new injection-reachability check (MPI-300 follow-up), not by a user report.

BUG: in comfy_workflows/wan22_i2v_stage2.json the `Input_Seed` node (717) has ZERO consumers, and sampler 733 (SamplerCustom) carries a BAKED `noise_seed: 0`. So every WAN i2v stage-2 finish runs on seed 0 instead of the run's seed. The app injects Input_Seed by title, the value lands in an orphan node, and nothing errors.

PROOF (twin comparison, same node ids): wan22_t2v_stage2.json has 717 -> 733 with `noise_seed` LINKED to it. wan22_i2v_stage2.json does not. The t2v twin is the reference for the correct wiring.

IMPACT: stage-2 is the finishing sampler, so i2v results are less varied than they should be across runs. Pre-existing; unclear how long.

FIX (USER — graph edit, agents never edit workflow JSON): open the wan22_i2v raw template in ComfyUI, connect Input_Seed (717) to sampler 733's noise_seed exactly as the t2v twin does, re-export, then `node scripts/sync-raw-workflows.mjs`.

VERIFY: `node scripts/validate-injection-rules.mjs comfy_workflows/wan22_i2v_stage2.json` must pass (it currently fails on this), and a repeat i2v run should differ.

NOTE: the raw source for the stage-2 file needs checking — stage-2 files are baked by orchestrate.py from wan22_i2v_template.json, so the fix belongs in the TEMPLATE, not the generated file.

RESOLVED — NOT A BUG (closed 2026-07-18).

Sampler 733 in BOTH twins runs `add_noise: False`. SamplerCustom with add_noise disabled never samples noise, so `noise_seed` is dead input — the baked 0 in the i2v twin has zero effect on output. The t2v twin's 717->733 link is equally inert; it is not the 'correct wiring' the card claimed, just a link that happens to exist.

Stage 2 is a refiner continuing stage 1's nearly-finished latent. All run-to-run variance comes from stage 1's seed, which IS wired. Changing a stage-2 seed changes nothing in the generation; the only thing that meaningfully alters stage-2 output is a LoRA change. So there is no reason for a seed here at all.

No graph edit needed — the USER fix described above is withdrawn. Residue: the Input_* reachability check in validate-injection-rules.mjs flags Input_Seed (717) as an orphan. That is a linter false positive on a legitimately unused injection node, not a generation defect. If it re-files this card, teach the check that a noise_seed on an `add_noise: False` sampler is not a live consumer.
